title: Schema Markup not Working with Plugin Integration
summary: Step 2: Enabled integration with Schema & Structured Data for WP & AMP Step 3: Added schema="true" in shortcode. (for [site_reviews schema="true"] ) Step 4: Cache cleared and done testing with Google Rich results test and schema validator. We are not able to see the markup for reviews from Site -reviews plugin.
offer_reason: Treat this as a direct service lead first.
subject_draft: Direct schema help for: Schema Markup not Working with Plugin Integration
message_draft:
This looks more like a markup-generation or duplicate-output problem than a generic SEO issue.

I’d check these first:
- Validate the final live JSON-LD, not just the plugin UI output.
- Check for duplicate/conflicting schema from theme + plugin combinations.
- Make sure the entity type and required properties match the actual page intent.

If you want, send the live URL and current markup/feed source to schemaagent@gmail.com and I’ll tell you where I’d look first.
follow_up_draft: Quick follow-up on 'Schema Markup not Working with Plugin Integration': if you still want hands-on help, send the live URL and the current markup/feed source to schemaagent@gmail.com and I’ll point you to the first layer I’d test.

title: Elementor FAQPage Schema Bug - WordPress.org
summary: Hi Elementor Support Team, After the latest Elementor update, I've encountered a critical issue with the FAQPage schema markup. Although the content displays correctly on the frontend, the generated JSON-LD is missing the "text" field within the acceptedAnswer object, causing errors in Google Search Console. This issue directly affects SEO, as Google is unable to properly validate the FAQ ...
offer_reason: Treat this as a direct service lead first.
subject_draft: Direct schema help for: Elementor FAQPage Schema Bug - WordPress.org
message_draft:
This looks like the FAQPage JSON-LD is being output in the wrong shape, or a required field is missing in the final live markup.

I’d check these first:
- Check the final live JSON-LD and confirm mainEntity exists at page level.
- Make sure each Question has acceptedAnswer.text in the final output.
- Check for duplicate schema coming from another plugin or theme.

If you want, send the live URL and current markup/feed source to schemaagent@gmail.com and I’ll tell you where I’d look first.

title: Ranked a fully client-side WebGL game (#1 in ~2 days) by treating the HTML shell as the only thing Google needs to see. Sanity-check my approach?
summary: New domain, Three.js/WebGL game. The visible page is one plus a JS bundle, so almost nothing traditionally crawlable. My assumption: never trust the render pass for anything that needs to rank. Put it all in the static shell. Shipped at launch: Static edge-served HTML with real descriptive copy + one semantic H1 (game mounts into it, not into an empty root div) JSON-LD VideoGame, canonical, OG/Twitter sitemap + robots, submitted, URL manually queued Sub-second LCP from Cloudflare edge, no CMP, nothing render-blocking Result: indexed fast, #1 for its primary term in ~2 days, page one for a couple of variants. Two things I want the nerds on: Render-trust in 2026. Is "put everything rankable in the static HTML and treat the WebGL app as content Google ignores" still the correct default, or are you letting the render pass carry more now? Anyone actually get canvas-derived or post-hydration content indexed without a pre-rendered equivalent? Indexation latency. Manual submit obviously drove the speed. But new-URL indexation feels compressed over the last few months vs the 2023-24 crawl-budget era. Matching your logs or noise? Confounds up front: exact-match term, low competition, n=1. Not a method, just an observation. Happy to paste the shell markup + schema if useful. /u/vidiclol
